source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An equilateral triangle with side length 3 and a circle with radius 1 have the same centre. What is the perimeter of the figure that is created when the two are being put together?
<image1>
extraction_notes: Answer field is an option letter and was mapped to the corresponding option text.
Ignored decoded_image because it is not a path/URL.

image_dependency: The task is about the perimeter of a combined geometric figure formed by an equilateral triangle and a circle with the same centre, which is strongly tied to spatial overlap and visible boundary structure.
An image asset is present and registry_passed=true, indicating the visual is part of the intended sample.
The question text does include the key measurements in words, so image dependence is high but not absolute.
multi_step: Solving requires identifying which parts of the triangle and circle contribute to the outer perimeter of the combined figure, not just applying a single direct formula.
The reasoning must combine multiple givens: equilateral triangle side length 3, circle radius 1, and same centre.
The expected exact-form answer suggests decomposition into linear segments and circular arc length before summing.
verifiability: The provided answer is an exact symbolic expression ('$6+\pi$'), which is straightforward to compare against a derived result.
There are 5 multiple-choice options, so correctness can also be checked by option match.

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no issues, and core assets are complete: question, answer, and image are all present
initial scoring is strong overall, with high multi-step and verifiability scores
the geometry problem has clear recoverable semantics from both text and image, reducing asset risk
the only noted risk flag indicates the text already contains core givens, which does not undermine candidate usability
